description.current: AI-augmented evidence review: vision models analyze screenshots, LLMs check API/CLI output. Produces 0-100 confidence scores and findings. Optional in offline mode.
issues: Description omits WHO uses this (verdict-writer agent) and WHERE in pipeline (phase 3.5 between execute and analyze)
Doesn't mention the 3 evidence types supported or what 'confidence score' actionably enables (prioritization, finding detection)

issues.description: Massive feature bloat: 354 lines covering vision + LLM + JSON schema + edge cases + disable logic + aggregation rules. Should be split into minimal skill + 4 bundled references. Right now a user reading this can't quickly find 'how do I run AI analysis on my evidence?'
Schema documentation (lines 52-84) is exhaustive but unclear: AnalysisResult JSON structure takes 30 lines; could be 10 lines with a link to references/analysis-schema.md

Analysis protocol (lines 112-240) mixes 5 step-by-step instructions with complex CLI snippets (lines 117-152 for file detection). Users trying to do step 1 have to wade through line 140s bash one-liners with xxd and grep
Evidence type classification (lines 124-152) repeats extension logic then 'fallback content inspection' — this is a bundled script opportunity (detect_evidence_type.sh), not inline education

Confidence score tiers (lines 86-94) presented inline, but the full scoring rubric lives in references/confidence-scoring.md (409 lines). Bidirectional references create cognitive overhead — users can't tell which is authoritative
Disable/offline logic (lines 276-323) uses 3 different disable methods (env var, config flag, implicit offline detection). No single clear 'how to disable' sentence before diving into methods
Anti-patterns section (lines 338-346) is good guardrails but comes after 200+ lines of detail — should come after 'When to Use', not at end

strengths: Crystal clear Scope section (lines 28-42): what it does and what it does NOT do — good boundary setting
Supported evidence types table (lines 44-50) is scannable and includes analysis model and example checks
Vision/LLM prompt templates (lines 164-205) are pragmatic — users can copy directly to Claude
Bundled references are well-organized: confidence-scoring.md (409 lines), screenshot-analysis.md, api-response-analysis.md, cli-output-analysis.md
Journey-level aggregation (lines 251-273) explains weighted averaging clearly with worked example
Integration callout (lines 325-336) shows where this fits in 7-phase pipeline and how verdict-writer uses output

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: Evidence type detection (lines 124-152) with xxd/file/json checks could be detect-evidence-type.sh; Analysis protocol Step 1 (lines 116-119) find+sort command could be discover-evidence.sh; both would make main skill less CLI-heavy

top_fixes: Split into 2 files: (1) Minimal SKILL.md (<120 lines): When to Use, Quick Start (run analysis on e2e-evidence/, get confidence scores), Integration section. (2) References: full Analysis Protocol, Schema details, Confidence scoring (already bundled), Anti-patterns. Current lines 52-240 move to references/analysis-protocol.md.

Rewrite description: 'Intelligent analysis of validation evidence using vision models (screenshots) and LLMs (API/CLI output). Produces confidence scores (0-100) and findings that inform verdict decisions. Runs at phase 3.5 (between capture and analysis). Optional in offline mode.' (Target 40-50 words)
Move evidence classification logic (lines 124-152) to scripts/detect-evidence-type.sh + reference in main skill as 'use references/detect-evidence-type.sh to classify evidence files'

Collapse schema documentation: replace lines 52-84 with: 'Every evidence item produces AnalysisResult (see references/analysis-schema.json for full schema). Key fields: evidence_file, confidence (0-100), verdict_label (PASS/WARN/FAIL), findings[].'

Move disable/offline logic to a separate section right after 'When to Use': 'Disable checks: set VF_AI_ANALYSIS=disabled or config ai_analysis.enabled=false. Offline mode skips all API calls; pipeline continues without analysis.'
Move anti-patterns (lines 338-346) to come immediately after 'When to Use' as 'Common mistakes'

Create references/quick-start-example.md showing end-to-end: 'You have 5 evidence files in e2e-evidence/login-journey/. Run AI analysis, get confidence scores, save results to ai-analysis-*.json. Copy findings to verdict report.'
Keep the 4 bundled references (confidence-scoring.md, screenshot-analysis.md, api-response-analysis.md, cli-output-analysis.md) — they are excellent

test_prompts.prompt: I captured 3 pieces of evidence for my login journey: login-page.png (screenshot), auth-response.json (API response), and build-log.txt (CLI output). I want AI to analyze all 3 and tell me if they all support a PASS verdict. What confidence scores should I be looking for?
test_prompts.expected_output: Run AI analysis on all 3 files. Get individual confidence scores: screenshot should be 85+, API response should be 75+, build log should be 70+. Aggregate to journey-level score. If all are 70+, journey-level is probably 75+ (PASS). If any has CRITICAL findings, journey score drops to 50 (FAIL).

test_prompts.prompt: I'm in an air-gapped environment (no API access). Can I still use this skill? What happens to the confidence scores?
test_prompts.expected_output: Set VF_AI_ANALYSIS=disabled. Skill skips all model API calls, doesn't produce analysis files, logs one notice line. Verdict writer proceeds with raw evidence only. No confidence scores generated, but validation continues.

test_prompts.prompt: My screenshot shows a loading spinner on the 'Create Order' button. The skill gave it a confidence of 52. Why is that, and can I still use it as evidence?
test_prompts.expected_output: Loading spinner = incomplete page load = low confidence (50-69 tier). Score is 52 because page is NOT fully rendered. Use is risky — the feature might be broken, or just slow. You need to capture a follow-up screenshot after the page fully loads to get high-confidence evidence.

test_prompts.prompt: Our API response includes an old deprecated field 'legacy_token' that our team plans to remove next quarter. The AI analysis flagged it as a finding with LOW severity and scored the response 87. Should we still PASS this journey?
test_prompts.expected_output: Score is 87 (MODERATE tier, PASS eligible) because the deprecated field is LOW severity and doesn't block the feature. Verdict should be PASS with a note: 'One deprecated field detected; plan removal in Q2'. The confidence score and findings are input to the verdict, not the verdict itself.
